title: All Praise to Jesus' Hallowed Name
abc_source:
X: 48
T: All Praise to Jesus' Hallowed Name
%%combinevoices 1
T: (also known as All Praise to Thee, O Jesus Christ or O Jesus Christ, All Praise
T: to Thee or We Praise You, Jesus at Your Birth or All Praise to Thee, Eternal Lord)
C: Words: verse 1, ancient German.  verses 2-7, Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Richard Massie, 1854, alt. 
C: Music: 'Gelobet Seist Du' ancient German found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524. 
C: Setting: Karl August Haupt, 1869.
C: copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.
S: Music source: "The Hymns of Martin Luther by Leonard Woolsey Bacon 1883, p. 20.

W: 6. He came to earth so mean and poor, Man to pity and restore,
W: And make us rich in heaven above, Equal with angels through his love.
W: 
W: 7. All this He did to show His grace To our poor and sinful race;
W: For this let Christendom adore And praise His name for evermore.

metadata.composer: 'Gelobet Seist Du' ancient German found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524 Karl August Haupt, 1869
metadata.lyricist: verse 1, ancient German.  verses 2-7, Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Richard Massie, 1854, alt
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.
c_lines: Words: verse 1, ancient German.  verses 2-7, Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Richard Massie, 1854, alt.
Music: 'Gelobet Seist Du' ancient German found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.

1.raw_text: All praise to Jesus' hallowed Name Who of virgin pure became True man for us! The angels sing As the glad news to earth they bring. Hallelujah!

2.raw_text: Th'Eternal Father's only Son For a manger leaves His throne Disguised in our poor flesh and blood See now the everlasting Good.

3.raw_text: He whom the world could not inwrap Yonder lies in Mary's lap; He is become an infant small, Who by His might upholdeth all.

4.raw_text: Th'Eternal Light, come down from heav'n, Hath to us new sunshine giv'n; It shineth in the midst of night, And maketh us the sons of light.

5.raw_text: The Father's Son, God ever\- blest, In the world became a guest; He leads us from this vale of tears, And makes us in his kingdom heirs.
